title: The People You'd Choose
prose: Third year has a way of sorting friendships from acquaintances — the people who stuck around through two years of homework, house points, and the ordinary friction of sharing a dormitory turn out, mostly, to be the ones worth keeping.
It's a quieter kind of milestone than a Quidditch match or an exam, but a real one: less {{name}} the first-year who'd take anyone's company, more {{name}} who actually knows who they'd choose.

choices.label: You're grateful for them, and say so.
You notice it, and leave it unsaid.
